Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(1998) (Gorillaz is formed by Damon Albarn & Jamie Hewlett)

Leak Date: The first album by Gorillaz following the Tomorrow Comes Today EP. The album was made in collaboration with Deltron 3030 members Dan the Automator (also of Handsome Boy Modelling School, Lovage, etc.) and Kid Koala, with Del the Funky Homosapien featuring on "Clint Eastwood" and "Rock the House".

Name:
Demon Days
(prod. Gorillaz & Danger Mouse)
(Celebrity Harvest, We Are Happy Landfill)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(March 26, 2001) & (December 12, 2001) (Gorillaz & G-Sides are officially released)
(May 11, 2005) & (November 19, 2007) (Demon Days & D-Sides are officially released)
Leak Date: With the goal of proving Gorillaz wasn’t a gimmick, Demon Days featured a noticeable shift in sound from the dub-influenced underground hip-hop of Gorillaz to a more political rock, pop, choral and hip-hop route brought on by string arrangements, drum-sequencing, and lyrics crticizing the Afghanistan War and life post-911. Co-produced by then-newcomer Danger Mouse, Demon Days is also the first Gorillaz album to heavily feature other artists and rely on collaboration, a future mainstay for all Gorillaz projects.

Name:
🏆 Celebrity Harvest [Film & Soundtrack]
(Scrapped Full-Length Gorillaz Animated Project* [V1])
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: One of three Gorillaz film scripts whose writing was led by Cass Browne, writer of all Gorillaz storylines/lore up until "DoYaThing". Celebrity Harvest was written in collaboration with the at-the-time writers for either South Park or The Simpsons. Plot was to revolve around boils that begin to appear on celebrities with egos who got too big, causing them to pop and infect others. Russel would go insane and leave the band, Murdoc would also leave and start a heavy metal band who would later become cannibals and turn Murdoc into a zombie. Del would appear again, turning evil after being exorcized out of Russel. The film was scrapped after Dreamworks wanted to make it a PG-13 family friendly movie, and issues with structuring the 360 page script. Visual elements from Celebrity Harvest would be re-used throughout Phase 2, while the album would be re-worked into Demon Days.

Name: Carousel
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(May 11, 2005) & (November 19, 2007) (Demon Days & D-Sides officially releases)
(January 14, 2009) (Damon plays three demos on BBC Radio 1)
Leak Date: Announced in 2008 as the third Gorillaz album, Carousel was to be a multi-media orchestral art-rock project contained in 15 stories. Thematically, Carousel would revolve around the mystical aspects of Britain and the progression of life all taking place on a 100-mile pier. Carousel was scrapped by EMI who refused to fund the project while Damon and Jamie struggled to structure the project. Damon and Jamie would then carry over elements from Carousel to another Gorillaz project, Plastic Beach.

The album portion of Carousel was to be recorded with an in-house band, either Hypnotic Brass Ensemble or sinfonia ViVA as confirmed by André de Ridder.

Could likely be an alternate version of the "Carousel" song played during the show for The Good The Bad and the Queen.

Name: Unknown [Demos for André de Ridder]
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
In May 2009, Damon would give German conductor André de Ridder 60 songs from Carousel with the intent of having de Ridder's sinfonia ViVA record for the songs. Eight songs were ultimately finished, wih only three being released. Existence not 100% confirmed, but the vast majority of Plastic Beach material is confirmed by Damon and Jamie to have originated from the Carousel project.
Songs known to have credits from sinfonia ViVA include: Apple Carts; A Very Dark Night; Cloud of Unknowing; Pirate's Progress; Three Hearts, Seven Seas, Twelve Moons & United Summer.

Name: Plastic Beach
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(January 14, 2009) (Damon plays three demos on BBC Radio 1)
(March 3, 2010) (Plastic Beach officially releases)
Leak Date: Conceived initially as three separate projects: Carousel, Plastic Beach and Dee, The Opera. The third Gorillaz album took on a more environmental focus from Damon observing the amounts of plastic on the sands of a beach. Plastic Beach is infamous for going over the band's budget, and its scrapped content, leading to the 2018 #FreeTheSea campaign calling for the release of the 200+ unreleased songs recorded during the making of Phase 3.

Name:
🏆 Leviathan [V3]
(feat. Gruff Rhys & The Horrors)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: Song was about the Leviathan whale / about a man having paranoid delusions about a whale trying to kill him. Rhys described the song as "more of a night-time song, a three o'clock in the morning, speeding down the Autobahn evading West German police-type track". A LQ snippet was previewed on 'The Making of Plastic Beach' DVD. Base-less rumours claim it's 12 minutes long. Cass Browne later revealed that Damon removed the song from the tracklist as he felt that "Glitter Freeze" fit "that section" of Plastic Beach.

Name:
Stylo [Music Video] [V1]
(feat. Yasiin Bey & Bobby Womack)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
Revealed by Jamie in an interview with Gorillaz Unofficial for their Plastic Beach fanzine.
Scrapped intro to the video depicting Cyborg Noodle kidnapping Bobby Womack to force him to record for Plastic Beach, instigating the events of the Stylo video. Mos Def was to portray The Boogeyman/Sun, Moon and Stars in the video. The full storyboard for the intro sequence is seen in The Making of Plastic Beach.

Name:
To Binge [Music Video]
(feat. Little Dragon)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: Revealed by Jamie in an interview with Gorillaz Unofficial for their Plastic Beach fanzine. Was to be the fourth video in the Plastic Beach chronology; Stylo, On Melancholy Hill, Rhinestone Eyes, To Binge. Was to be a fight between Noodle and Cyborg Noodle. The "Book of Gorillaz" promo series before the release of Humanz was confirmed by Jamie to re-use the end of the video, having the band split up and going their seperate ways again. Unknown if it was ever finished.

Name: The Fall
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(December 25, 2010) & (April 19, 2011) (The Fall officially releases)
(April 2012) (Gorillaz enter three-year hiatus)
Leak Date: "The Fall" is the fourth album by Gorillaz, infamous for being concieved on tour, on an iPad. It was first released as a download for members of the Gorillaz fan club on December 25th, 2010, followed by a wider physical release on April 19th, 2011 by Parlophone Records.

Name:
Crashing Down [V4]
(feat. Asha Bhosle & The Syrian National Orchestra for Arabic Music)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: In July 2010, Indian singer Asha Bhosle was to replace Engelbert Humperdinck (who never recorded his vocals) on the release version of "Crashing Down", as Damon seemingly wanted someone other than himself doing the vocals, or to have the song recorded as a duet. Damon not wanting himself on lead vocals would be done on the version from the 2016 documentary The White Helmets.

(Transformerz)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(January 30, 2015) (Jamie Hewlett posts new artwork of Murdoc & Noodle on Instagram)
(April 27, 2017) (Humanz officially releases)
Leak Date: After reuniting at a party, Damon and Jamie would begin work on a Gorillaz comeback album in 2015 titled Transformerz. After producer The Twilite Tone became involved in 2016, Transformerz would be renamed to Humanz, with a new concept imagining a party at the end of the world caused by (the then-hypothetical of) Donald Trump winning the 2016 US presidential election.

Name:

(prod. Gorillaz, James Ford & Remi Kabaka Jr.)
(3-D)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(April 27, 2017) (Humanz officially releases)
(June 29, 2018) (The Now Now officially releases)
Leak Date: Recorded during the Humanz Tour with the intention of having more Damon-centric songs to play at concerts after the reaction to his absence on the majority of Humanz. The Now Now is a largely funk-inspired album written from the perspective of 2-D, with the change in sound being accredited in-universe to the temporary substitution of Murdoc to Ace from The Powerpuff Girls as the band's bassist.

Name:

(Almanac CD)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(June 29, 2018) (The Now Now officially releases)
(October 23, 2020) (Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez officially releases)
Leak Date: Setting out to not make an album, Damon, Jamie, and longtime collaborator and newly credited Gorillaz member Remi Kabaka Jr began work on releasing singles accompanied by music videos near-monthly throughout 2020 in a series titled Song Machine. However, after the rise of the COVID-19 pandemic in March, more songs would be created through Zoom and emails, leading to the first season of Song Machine being expanded into an album released as Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez.

Name:
Scrapped Gorillaz Netflix Project*
(Scrapped Full-Length Gorillaz Animated Project [V4])
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: In the works since late 2018, as announced by Jamie. In an interview from October 18, 2020, Damon revealed to La Tercera that Gorillaz had made a deal with Netflix to produce a full-length, animated, one hour, feature Gorillaz film. The film was stated to be released after the second season of Song Machine, and that a first draft of the script had begun being written. In 2021, Damon would reveal that the movie's length had now reached an hour and 20 minutes in length and featured new and old Gorillaz music. On February 20, 2023 Damon would reveal that Netflix pulled out of the project due to them overfunding exclusive content.

Name: Meanwhile
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(October 23, 2020) (Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez officially releases)
(August 26, 2021) (Meanwhile officially releases)
Leak Date: The existence of new Gorillaz material after Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez would be revealed by Damon in an interview with NME describing it as "carnival-themed". Damon would later reveal in an interview with Mark Ronson that a new Gorillaz EP would be released to compliment that year's Notting Hill Carnival. Meanwhile was also released in celebration as a part of the 20th anniversary of Gorillaz, showcasing a modern take on the sounds explored 20 years ago on Gorillaz.

Name:

(prod. Gorillaz & Greg Kurstin)
(Song Machine, Season Two)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(August 26, 2021) (Meanwhile officially releases)
(February 24, 2023) (Cracker Island officially releases)
Leak Date: Originally announced as the second season of Song Machine in October 2020; Jamie, through the Gorillaz Unofficial 2020 Plastic Beach Fanzine, would reveal that the project following Song Machine would take on a narrative-heavy structure, describing it as a "more in-depth kinda epic story with the characters and music. Not just an album, but bigger." Cracker Island would topically revolve around cults and the Los Angeles lifestyle, where the album was recorded in 9-11 days with album collaborator Greg Kurstin.
